stem: A sign-up form asks the user to enter their age (0-120).
model_answer: Input validation checks that data entered by the user is sensible before it is processed. For an age 0-120 the program could use a range check (age is a number between 0 and 120 inclusive) and a type check (it must be a whole number). If validation fails the program should show an error message and ask the user to re-enter the value, so bad data never reaches the rest of the program.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe two validation checks that could be used for the age field and what happens if a check fails.

mark_points.text: range check - age is between 0 and 120 inclusive
accepted_alternatives: check age falls in allowed range

mark_points.text: type/format check - age is a whole number (integer), not text
accepted_alternatives: check input is numeric / presence check for a value

mark_points.text: if invalid: program rejects the value and prompts the user to re-enter
accepted_alternatives: error message shown and input requested again
